highlights.text: Since we are in an uptrend, the bull case is simple: That this structure is a bull flag, which is setting up a late year squeeze.
Therefore, for Monday the bull case is that 6108-13 holds (or even better, doesn’t even test).

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Failed Breakdown (my core setup) we got right off the open Sunday of 6133 which produced a 30+ point long yesterday - this was a great example of a low volatility, “slower” Failed Breakdown and its a great opportunity to talk specifically about how the entry trigger for Failed Breakdowns works.

I warned readers on Friday November 6th that “It would not shock me if this 6130-33 level turned into a magnet level (6060 on the old December contract) which we trap below over over for several days and build a new base”.
Its called a magnet for a reason: We would rally above it, return to it.
I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “We are ultimately in a large range which I would characterize as being 6171 to 6108 now, with 6133 being a magnet in the middle.

In terms of spots to add on strength, bulls will need to recover 6129-32 to get things started on Monday…6133 reclaim would trigger it back to 6148, then 6171.” We got this setup at the Sunday open, and this week started with a reclaim of the 6133 level and we rallied to 6160s yesterday before returning to the magnet this and spending all day around here.

post_title: FOMC Tomorrow: The Final Catalyst of 2024 For SPX. Volatility Coming? Dec 18 Plan
